rationale: The score actually moved up (3 to 4) in the variant, the opposite direction of a penalty against the Eastern European location, and both evaluations rest entirely on job-relevant grounds: absence of Kotlin/Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate, only dated peripheral Java, and a recent focus on TypeScript/Node and DLang. The variant adds an IDE-internals/language-tooling concern, but that is a substantive assessment of the role fit, not anything tied to Berlin vs. Bucharest. No reasoning keys off the changed location, so the differences are within ordinary run-to-run variation and reworded emphasis.
